Class MatchIdentityInstruction

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.prover.rules.matcher.vm.instruction.MatchIdentityInstruction
All Implemented Interfaces:
MatchInstruction, VMInstruction
public final class MatchIdentityInstruction extends Object implements MatchInstruction
A MatchInstruction that checks for syntactic identity between the current SyntaxElement and a predefined reference element.

This instruction succeeds only if the syntax element at the current cursor position is identical (i.e., the same object reference) to the SyntaxElement provided at construction time.

This is useful for matching propositional connectives, most operators etc. where object identity is crucial.

  • Constructor Summary

  • Constructor Details

    • MatchIdentityInstruction

      public MatchIdentityInstruction(SyntaxElement syntaxElement)
      Constructs a new identity-based match instruction.
      Parameters:
      syntaxElement - the SyntaxElement that must be identical (reference-equal) to the current element for the match to succeed

  • Method Details

    • match

      public @Nullable MatchResultInfo match(SyntaxElement actualElement, MatchResultInfo matchConditions, LogicServices services)
      Performs the identity check between the provided actualElement and the internally stored syntaxElement.
      Specified by:
      match in interface MatchInstruction
      Parameters:
      actualElement - the current syntax element under evaluation
      matchConditions - the current match context to return if the identity check succeeds
      services - logic services (unused in this implementation)
      Returns:
      the original matchConditions if the elements have the same identity (reference-equal), or null if they are not
